framework.title: How to answer an ML system design question
steps: Clarify requirements (what decision does this serve? who uses it?)
Serving & scaling (batching, caching, autoscale, warm floor)
Monitoring & drift (the four layers, ground-truth lag)
Cost (measure → cache → compress → route → quantize → distill)

architectures.title: Production RAG system
architectures.blurb: Ingestion → chunking → embedding → vector store → retriever → reranker → LLM → citation → cache → eval; freshness & guardrails.

architectures.title: Agentic RAG / multi-agent system
architectures.blurb: Planner, tool router, retrievers, critic/corrective loop (CRAG/Self-RAG), memory, MCP tools; bounded loops, idempotency, tracing.

architectures.title: LLM serving platform
architectures.blurb: Gateway → router → vLLM (PagedAttention + continuous batching) → KV-cache mgmt → autoscaling with warm floor; TTFT/TPOT SLOs.

architectures.title: Multi-tenant LLM gateway
architectures.blurb: Routing (small/big model), semantic + exact + prefix caching, rate limiting, cost metering, guardrails, observability.

architectures.title: Recommendation system
architectures.blurb: Two-tower retrieval + ranking; candidate gen → ranking → re-rank; feedback loops & position bias.

architectures.title: Real-time fraud detection
architectures.blurb: Streaming features + feature store + low-latency scoring + threshold/calibration + drift; PR-AUC focus, base-rate trap.

architectures.title: Feature store architecture
architectures.blurb: Offline (warehouse) + online (KV) + materialization + point-in-time joins; train/serve skew prevention.

architectures.title: Semantic / hybrid search
architectures.blurb: BM25 + dense + cross-encoder rerank; ANN index; evaluation (NDCG, recall@k).

architectures.title: ML monitoring & drift platform
architectures.blurb: Four layers, reference windows, PSI/KS, alert design, ground-truth reconciliation.

architectures.title: Distributed training platform
architectures.blurb: Orchestration, FSDP/ZeRO, checkpointing, experiment tracking (MLflow), fault tolerance.
